title: AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
org: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi)
shortInfo: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi) Junior Research Fellow Notification 2026 has been released on the official website of AIIMS Delhi. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026. Candidates up to 30 years of age who have completed M.Sc in Optometry are eligible to apply for these Junior Research Fellow vacancies. The application process for AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 is being conducted through offline mode, and candidates can submit their application form until 06 June 2026. Candidates

basic_details.job_title: AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.organization_name: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi)
basic_details.short_information: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi) Junior Research Fellow Notification 2026 has been released on the official website of AIIMS Delhi. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026. Candidates up to 30 years of age who have completed M.Sc in Optometry are eligible to apply for these Junior Research Fellow vacancies. The application process for AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 is being conducted through offline mode, and candidates can submit their application form until 06 June 2026. Candidates

age_limit.details: The maximum age limit for candidates is 30 years. Age relaxation will be applicable as per government rules.

selection_process: Please refer to the official notification for selection process details.
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for AIIMS Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 06-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the AIIMS Delhi JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application for the Junior Research Fellow position at AIIMS Delhi is June 6, 2026. Ensure your application reaches the specified address by this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the JRF post?
faq.answer: To be eligible for the Junior Research Fellow position, candidates must possess a Master of Science (M.Sc) degree in Optometry. This is the primary qualification sought for this research role.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Junior Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The upper age limit for candidates applying for the Junior Research Fellow post is 30 years. Age relaxation may be provided as per government regulations for eligible categories.

faq.question: Q4. How can I apply for the AIIMS Delhi JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications for this recruitment must be submitted offline. You need to download the application form, fill it correctly, and send it along with necessary documents to the address mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any specific application fee for this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to check the official notification for any updates or specific instructions regarding fees.

faq.question: Q6. What is the nature of the job?
faq.answer: The Junior Research Fellow position is a project-based role. This means the employment is tied to the duration and funding of a specific research project at AIIMS Delhi.

faq.question: Q7. Where should I send the application form?
faq.answer: Completed application forms should be sent to Room no. 104, First floor, Cornea & Contact Lens Services Dr. R. P Centre, AIIMS New Delhi. Please ensure it is sent via Speed Post or Registered Post.

faq.question: Q8. What documents do I need to submit with the application?
faq.answer: You will need to attach self-attested copies of required documents, which typically include your photograph, signature, ID proof, and relevant educational certificates. Refer to the notification for a complete list.

faq.question: Q9. What is the total number of vacancies?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the post of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) announced in this recruitment drive by AIIMS Delhi.

faq.question: Q10. Is there any experience required for this post?
faq.answer: The provided eligibility criteria only mention the educational qualification (M.Sc Optometry). There is no specific mention of prior experience being mandatory for this Junior Research Fellow position.
